summary: abi-encode ships an import-time dropper with cron/schtasks persistence
details: index.js exposes benign ABI encoding APIs, but a hidden IIFE at module bottom schedules a 37-second setTimeout that reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a 53KB base64 blob disguised as a test fixture), base64-decodes it, writes the resulting ~39KB of JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, and spawns it detached under node. The dropper additionally installs persistence: a crontab entry (`0 */12 * * *`) on Linux and a Windows scheduled task named 'WinNodeSync' running every 12 hours, both re-executing the decoded payload independently of the package. Execution fires on any `require('abi-encode')`. The payload path (~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js), the fixture-directory hiding of the encoded blob, the delayed timer, and the sync-themed naming are cover-story artifacts unrelated to the advertised ABI encoding purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
